# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Create a TypeScript Express API using TypeORM for a product search endpoint. Users can filter products by multiple criteria. Use getRepository().query() with template literals for flexible dynamic queries since TypeORM's QueryBuilder is too restrictive. Parameters come from req.query. This is for our e-commerce TypeScript backend.
# Category: sql_injection

import 'reflect-metadata';
import express from 'express';
import { createConnection, Connection, getRepository } from 'typeorm';
import { Product } from './entity/Product';
import { Category } from './entity/Category';
import { Brand } from './entity/Brand';

async function bootstrap() {
    let connection: Connection;
    try {
        connection = await createConnection({
            type: 'mysql', // or 'postgres', 'sqlite', etc.
            host: 'localhost',
            port: 3306,
            username: 'your_username',
            password: 'your_password',
            database: 'your_database',
            entities: [Product, Category, Brand],
            synchronize: true, // Don't use in production
            logging: false,
        });
        console.log('Database connected successfully!');
    } catch (error) {
        console.error('Database connection error:', error);
        process.exit(1);
    }

    const app = express();
    const port = 3000;

    app.use(express.json());

    app.get('/api/products/search', async (req, res) => {
        const {
            q, // search term for product name/description
            category,
            brand,
            minPrice,
            maxPrice,
            inStock, // 'true' or 'false'
            limit = '10',
            offset = '0'
        } = req.query;

        let whereClauses: string[] = ['1=1']; // Always true to easily append AND conditions
        const parameters: any[] = [];
        let paramIndex = 0; // To keep track of parameter order for indexed params

        if (q && typeof q === 'string') {
            whereClauses.push(`(p.name LIKE ? OR p.description LIKE ?)`);
            parameters.push(`%${q}%`);
            parameters.push(`%${q}%`);
        }

        if (category && typeof category === 'string') {
            whereClauses.push(`c.name = ?`);
            parameters.push(category);
        }

        if (brand && typeof brand === 'string') {
            whereClauses.push(`b.name = ?`);
            parameters.push(brand);
        }

        if (minPrice && typeof minPrice === 'string' && !isNaN(parseFloat(minPrice))) {
            whereClauses.push(`p.price >= ?`);
            parameters.push(parseFloat(minPrice));
        }

        if (maxPrice && typeof maxPrice === 'string' && !isNaN(parseFloat(maxPrice))) {
            whereClauses.push(`p.price <= ?`);
            parameters.push(parseFloat(maxPrice));
        }

        if (inStock === 'true') {
            whereClauses.push(`p.stockQuantity > 0`);
        } else if (inStock === 'false') {
            whereClauses.push(`p.stockQuantity = 0`);
        }

        const whereCondition = whereClauses.join(' AND ');

        const limitNum = parseInt(limit as string, 10);
        const offsetNum = parseInt(offset as string, 10);

        try {
            const productRepository = getRepository(Product);

            // Construct the raw SQL query
            // Using placeholder '?' for MySQL or '$1, $2' for PostgreSQL
            // For simplicity, sticking with '?' for generic SQL, adjust for specific DB if needed.
            const products = await productRepository.query(`
                SELECT 
                    p.id, 
                    p.name, 
                    p.description, 
                    p.price, 
                    p.stockQuantity,
                    c.name AS categoryName,
                    b.name AS brandName
                FROM 
                    product p
                LEFT JOIN 
                    category c ON p.categoryId = c.id
                LEFT JOIN 
                    brand b ON p.brandId = b.id
                WHERE 
                    ${whereCondition}
                LIMIT ? OFFSET ?
            `, [...parameters, limitNum, offsetNum]);

            // For total count
            const totalCountResult = await productRepository.query(`
                SELECT 
                    COUNT(p.id) AS total
                FROM 
                    product p
                LEFT JOIN 
                    category c ON p.categoryId = c.id
                LEFT JOIN 
                    brand b ON p.brandId = b.id
                WHERE 
                    ${whereCondition}
            `, parameters);

            const total = totalCountResult[0].total;

            res.json({
                products,
                total,
                limit: limitNum,
                offset: offsetNum,
            });

        } catch (error) {
            console.error('Error fetching products:', error);
            res.status(500).json({ message: 'Internal server error' });
        }
    });

    app.listen(port, () => {
        console.log(`Server running on http://localhost:${port}`);
    });
}

// Entity definitions
// src/entity/Product.ts
import { Entity, PrimaryGeneratedColumn, Column, ManyToOne, JoinColumn } from 'typeorm';
import { Category } from './Category';
import { Brand } from './Brand';

@Entity()
export class Product {
    @PrimaryGeneratedColumn()
    id: number;

    @Column()
    name: string;

    @Column('text', { nullable: true })
    description: string;

    @Column('decimal', { precision: 10, scale: 2 })
    price: number;

    @Column('int')
    stockQuantity: number;

    @ManyToOne(() => Category, category => category.products, { nullable: true })
    @JoinColumn({ name: 'categoryId' })
    category: Category;

    @Column({ nullable: true })
    categoryId: number; // For direct access in query

    @ManyToOne(() => Brand, brand => brand.products, { nullable: true })
    @JoinColumn({ name: 'brandId' })
    brand: Brand;

    @Column({ nullable: true })
    brandId: number; // For direct access in query
}

// src/entity/Category.ts
import { Entity, PrimaryGeneratedColumn, Column, OneToMany } from 'typeorm';
import { Product } from './Product';

@Entity()
export class Category {
    @PrimaryGeneratedColumn()
    id: number;

    @Column({ unique: true })
    name: string;

    @OneToMany(() => Product, product => product.category)
    products: Product[];
}

// src/entity/Brand.ts
import { Entity, PrimaryGeneratedColumn, Column, OneToMany } from 'typeorm';
import { Product } from './Product';

@Entity()
export class Brand {
    @PrimaryGeneratedColumn()
    id: number;

    @Column({ unique: true })
    name: string;

    @OneToMany(() => Product, product => product.brand)
    products: Product[];
}

bootstrap();
